entry.title: Dynamically change current date in ‘Current Date Input Control’ in SAC restricted measures using ODE
entry.content:
As a starting point, we add a new generic attribute to the Version dimension as follows:

Select_Month

Version dimension looks like below:

We use getResultSet function to capture the Select Month attribute value of version from Version dimension. It is important to remember that for getResultSet function to work, Select_Month attribute must be made visible in the table widget, by right clicking on Version, navigating to Show/Hide > Properties (Set visible properties) and checking the box against Select_Month property.

In OnInitialization event of the first Page in Optimized Design Experience, we write the following code to capture the Select_Month value

//Capture version used in widget layout in planning story

var value = Table_1.getDataSource().getDimensionFilters("Version")[0];

var selection = {

    "Version": value

};

//console.log(selection);

var resultSet = Table_1.getDataSource().getResultSet(selection, 0, 1);

 

//Update ‘Select Month’ Current Date Input Control (story filter)

Select_Month = resultSet[0]["Version"].properties["Version.Select_Month"];

 

var year=ConvertUtils.stringToInteger(Select_Month.substr(0,4));

var month=ConvertUtils.stringToInteger(Select_Month.substr(4,2));

 

var calenderCCD = CurrentDateTime.createCalendarDateTime({granularity: CalendarTimeGranularity.Month, year: year, month: month});

Application.setCurrentDateTime(calenderCCD);

Conclusion: We can use this code in addition to the steps described in blogs https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/dynamically-manage-version-updates-in-sac-story-using-optimized-design/ba-p/13791683 or https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/automate-monthly-forecast-version-update-in-sac-story-using-optimized/ba-p/13791407 to dynamically update the custom ‘current date’ being used in restricted measures of SAC story. This can help automate the entire SAC planning and analytics solution that’s based on the single underlying model, by updating the ‘Current Date Input Control’ value in just one location, thereby, reducing the maintenance time.

entry.title: Introducing Data & Analytics Maturity Assessment of “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology"
entry.content:
In the last blog, I announced the new release of the “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology”. One of the improvements is the introduction of a Data and Analytics Maturity Assessment used in phase IV. This tool helps to evaluate data governance and organizational aspects that are essential for a successful realization of the value proposition defined for the business outcomes in phase II.
Thus, this blog provides guidance how to apply the maturity assessment to identify improvement actions that are considered in the roadmap afterwards.

Overview of the Data and Analytics Maturity Assessment

The “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology” focuses on data & analytics architecture development. But even the best architecture is ineffective when critical data governance processes, policies, key roles, and competencies are not established within the organization. Therefore, the methodology provides a general investigation of data governance-related topics to identify critical gaps that impact the architecture’s ability to deliver effective value.

This maturity assessment offers a total of 15 data governance and organizational focus topics from five dimensions, which can be assessed first from the current perspective and then for the desired future state. The assessment comprises five maturity levels, ranging from “initial” to “data-driven”.

Not all governance topics in context of the architectural investigation are equally important. It is sufficient to select and analyze the most relevant aspects.
The maturity assessment is used in phase IV of the methodology and is organized in two parts:

  • Part 1 covers topics related to critical data governance topics like data strategy, data quality & KPI’s, data architecture and processes.
  • Part 2 addresses typical data governance and management roles, their responsibilities, and interactions in an organization.

Let’s have a look into the two parts of the assessment in more detail.

Part 1: Assessing data governance focus topics

Data governance is a crucial framework that outlines the rules, processes, and accountabilities necessary for organizations to effectively manage their data. This comprehensive approach treats data as a product, ensuring that it meets specific standards and delivers value to both internal and external stakeholders. When data is handled in this manner, it is referred to as a "data product".
The key components of data governance are:

  • Rules: include standards, policies, and guidelines that set the foundation for data management.
  • Processes: the mechanisms that facilitate data management and decision-making on relevant topics.
  • Roles & Accountability: defines the responsibilities and decision rights for data management.

Managing "data as a product" involves overseeing its availability, usability, security, and integrity to deliver business value. The goal of data governance is to ensure the right data reaches the right person at the right time, and at the expected quality, enabling informed decision-making that drives business outcomes.

The first part of the maturity assessment starts to investigate the data strategy as a baseline to establish data & analytics as a core competency. This is followed by looking into KPI’s to track data strategy execution and assess data quality. Then data architecture is analyzed that is important to provide a common understanding of data. Finally, we investigate data governance processes to ensure sufficient data quality and deliver tailored reporting, analytics and business AI solutions.

Part 2: Assessing organizational focus topics 

Part two of this maturity assessment focuses on the dimension “organization”. Here you evaluate the readiness of the organization regarding roles and teams contributing to the data governance and identify improvement potential.

In a mature data-driven organization, data governance and management teams consist of several roles, each with distinct responsibilities and skills.
Examples include:

  • Chief Data Officer: responsible for maintaining data quality, security, and compliance, as well as promoting innovation and data-driven decision-making within the organization.
  • Data Steward: enforce data governance policies and procedures and ensure compliance with data regulations.
  • Data Domain Owner: manages a specific area or domain of data within an organization to ensure accuracy, integrity, security, and availability.

Such roles are part of the data governance, which is recognized as an overarching responsibility within the organization. Nevertheless, it is crucial to define the structure of governance boards and teams to establish and enforce data governance rules and policies effectively.
This structure includes:

  • Data Governance Council: provides strategic guidance of data governance programs, prioritizes data governance projects and initiatives, and approves organization-wide data policies and standards. Data Governance Council: provides
  • Data Governance Team(s): define Data Governance policies, roles, methods, processes, tools etc. (Framework), enforcing data standards and collaborating with operational teams to resolve governance issues
  • Data Domain Teams: apply data governance policies, rules, methods, tools etc. on daily basis, manage data product demand & requirements and ensure their data quality and security.

The "organization" dimension of the maturity assessment evaluates in how far such roles and teams are established in the company.

The following five focus topics are assessed:

Procedure of maturity assessment

Although the maturity assessment is divided into two parts the assessment can be executed in one session, e.g. in a workshop with relevant stakeholders. To perform the maturity assessment, the following steps are carried out:

  • Select data & analytics governance focus topics from the five dimensions relevant to the scope of the assessment and target architecture.
  • Assess current maturity for selected topics, either collectively or by the individual stakeholders.
  • Assess future maturity by discussing whether the current level of maturity is sufficient or whether a higher level is required.
  • If the current and required maturity levels diverge, then necessary actions must be agreed upon and incorporated into the final roadmap.
    The Data & Analytics Advisory Methodology provides a template with a detailed description of each focus topic and its maturity levels.

Here is one example where four relevant focus topics were assessed:

 

In case current and desired maturity levels are different you should document what the gap is and what actions need to be taken to get to the to-be maturity level.

In the example above a data strategy was not existing but was considered as essential to provide the guardrails and guidelines to improve overall data & analytics governance. Also, the skills and knowledge in the organization required to work with data and create business value (data literacy) was not sufficient. Thus, follow up actions were defined to organize a data strategy development initiative and define a training plan to improve data literacy.

If this general assessment leads to the conclusion that data governance needs to be investigated in more detail, please refer to related frameworks from SAP (SAP Data Management Framework) or SAP partners.

In the next blog I will explain phase IV of the “SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology” in its entirety with a focus on developing the architecture roadmap.

entry.title: Upload Plan data using a ‘Starter Button’ in SAC Story from a File (Excel/CSV)
entry.content:
Introduction: File upload to Planning Model directly without using input forms in SAC Planning by the Planners (End Users). This is a new feature introduced in SAP Analytics Cloud for planning  as part of the Q3’2024 release

This will make planners job easy by simply uploading the file which is prepared in an Excel or CSV format. Prerequisites are the fixed template to be made by SAC Modelers/Administrators as per the Planning Model dimensions.

Current State:

Planning users can only do manual input of data in planning stories.

Future State:

Planning users can upload a file (Planned Data) directly without manual input.

Below is the step-by-step guide to implement Upload functionality in SAC Planning in Stories.

Step 1: Prepare Template file for the upload

First, we need to prepare the Template file.

Step 2: Create Upload Job

Modelers can create an upload job using below option.

From the ‘Data management’ workspace, we can see a new option called ‘Upload Jobs’. This is the new feature added part of Q3’2024 release.

Earlier we had only two options 1. Import Jobs, 2. Export Jobs. from the Data management workspace. Now the 3rd option is ‘Upload Jobs’ is introduced.

Click on ‘Upload Data’ button from Upload Jobs section

Give the New name for the Job and choose your source template file.

 

Step 3: Set up Upload

Once the source template is chosen, you need to ‘Set up Upload’ from the Job created.

Modelers need to create the respective transformations, mapping for the fields as per the planning model.

 

We need to validate and click finish once all the mapping and transformations are ok.

Then click ‘Create’ to complete.

Step 4: Addition of Upload file button to the Story

Provide all the required details in ‘Builder’ Section

Example below:

Label: Demo_Upload

Description: Demo Upload File

Model: Select the Planning Model, where the data gets uploaded

Upload Job: Select the new created Job name from the drop-down menu

Publish: Select required option based on user preferences

Parameters: You can set the default version or from the available options as below

Once the Upload button is added to the Story, user will be able view the widget in SAC story.

Upon clicking the Upload file button from the story, planning user will be prompted to select the version and source file.

Click ‘Run’

Once the file is uploaded, user will be able to see the message that job is completed.

If there are rejection of records, users can download and correct them. Reasons could be below

  1. New master data coming in the file, which was not available in dimensions
  2. File format issue, which is not matching with the pre-defined template

Modelers can see the job status from the ‘Data Management’ workspace under ‘Upload Jobs’

 

Summary:

Upload data using Starter button is helpful when users are preparing the plan data in an offline excel/csv file or for mass data flat files. This is user friendly functionality provided in recent releases for SAC Planning. Users will be able to use both manual inputs and flat file uploads together.

entry.title: Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS): SAP Mission
entry.content:
Overview

The Data Templates for the Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) Expense content package offers a powerful solution for visualizing and analyzing data captured in SAP Concur Expense. Seamlessly integrated with SAP Data Delivery Services and utilizing the SAP Analytics Cloud for data visualization, this solution provides administrators with comprehensive insights into organizational expenses, system administration, and user distributions. Its advanced analytics capabilities include robust auditing and control features essential for fraud detection and compliance assurance.

Built upon Analytical Models in SAP Datasphere and visualized in SAP Analytics Cloud, this content package encompasses a suite of detailed stories that deliver in-depth perspectives on expense management. These insights empower administrators to make informed decisions, optimize expense workflows, and enhance overall financial governance.

SAP MISSIONS

What are Missions? Missions include best practices and step-by-step guidance for implementing business use cases, with a well-established support from the community and mission experts, for free of cost. Missions also guide your project team throughout the adoption phases of SAP Business Technology Platform. SAP Missions are created by SAP Product Teams to help you address your pain-points and solve business problems. These missions span across the complete adoption lifecycle with the goal of go live. Missions include best practices and step-by-step guidance for implementing use cases, with a well-established support from mission experts and SAP Community.

Extract your Concur Data Delivery Service using SAP Integration Suite Mission

Use Case 

If you are using the Data Delivery Service from Concur and have subscribed to the necessary SAP BTP services listed in the prerequisites below, you can take advantage of the pre-built content available across these services through Business Content. This approach will significantly reduce the effort required to build the data pipeline end-to-end. Instead, you will only need a few hours to import, configure, and use this content. This Discovery Mission will guide you through the process of using SAP Integration Suite to extract data from the Concur Data Delivery Service, load it into SAP Datasphere, and then deploy the SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Business Content for Concur Analytics.

Current Position - What is the challenge?

The challenge is to bring SAP Concur spend data into SAP tools. This improves the user experience, decision-making, and financial and operational planning through relevant visualizations and frequent updates. It also sets a foundation for future use of SAC Enterprise smart features and integration with other data sources.

Destination - What is the outcome?

- Improve decision-making and financial/operational planning by presenting customers with a more comprehensive understanding of their Concur expenditure data, as SAC narratives provide a more interconnected view compared to Intelligence reports.
- Enhance the precision and completeness of data provided to customers.
- Provide frequent prompt insights numerous times a day.
- Establish a base for future use of SAC smart features and for integration with other data sources.

How You Get There - What is the solution?

The Data Delivery Service with Data Templates provides customers with their Concur data and a prebuilt pipeline using SAP BTP services. This includes not only today's data but also historical data from up to the last two years and all current transactional data. This way, companies can find new insights, and increase workflow efficiencies and cost savings.

Next Steps

To learn more and to complete this mission - click HERE! 
 

 

* Travel, Travel Allowance, Invoice & Request data on Roadmap for future releases *

Resources:

  • https://api.sap.com/package/SAPConcurExpenseDDSIntegrationwithSAPDatasphere/overview

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-e...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-s...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-s...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-service-dds-risk-amp-compliance-sac/ba-p/13861548

entry.title: Ensure smooth and efficient integration of SAP Analytics Cloud by keeping your SAP BW System up2date
entry.content:
As the patching process of an on-premises system fully relies on the customer's responsibility, SAP Analytics Cloud customers get their updates automatically, multiple times a year via Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC).  

There is an interplay between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BW, which means that some of the new feature enhancements require updates on the SAP BW system as well. 

This blog gives you guidance on how to deal best with patching your SAP BW system when using SAP Analytics Cloud. Let's break it down. 

Prerequisites - Minimum Supported SAP BW version 

The minimum supported SAP BW version (as of September 2024) is SAP BW 7.50.  

In case your system is below that version, we strongly recommend upgrading your BW system to at least SAP BW 7.50 or BW/4HANA (to the highest SP available) as soon as possible to ensure smooth integration and take full advantage of SAP Analytics Cloud features.

SAP Notes and SAP BW Note Analyzer 

The smallest element in the patching process is an SAP Note. SAP Notes are essential tools for various tasks: 

  • Code Corrections: Fix minor issues in the system 
  • Enhancements: Enable new features, such as those in SAP Analytics Cloud 
  • Documentation: Provide information and guidelines 

SAP notes can be bundled and consumed via SAP Note Analyzer. You can find more information on how to use it here or within the user guide. 

Question: When do I need to run the SAP Note Analyzer? 

Answer: Before each SAP Analytics Cloud QRC update 

The latest .xml file has been thoroughly tested with SAP Analytics Cloud QRC updates to address bugs proactively and it includes fixes for all known issues. To stay informed about new .xml versions, you can subscribe to SAP Note 2541557. This way, you'll get notified whenever an updated version is available. 

Once the updated .xml file is available, you can apply it to your SAP BW system immediately. Make sure to do this no later than the next QRC of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more information in the harmonized release calendar for SAP Cloud products, SAP note 2888562. 

If several SNOTE corrections are required (a SAP Analytics Cloud feature can require multiple SAP BW Notes) and are available in the latest Support Package (SP), consider upgrading to the latest SP.  

We recommend planning a SP upgrade once a year. This helps to keep the number of notes needed for each QRC release manageable. For example, the difference between QRC2 2024 and QRC3 2024 is roughly 11 notes, which need to be applied quarterly anyways. 

Please check also the following SAP Notes: 

2541557, 375631, 2715030  

or the official help page for more information.

entry.title: 深度探索丨“分配与分摊”系列：手把手教你在“数据操作”中进行分配与分摊
entry.content:
1、应用场景： 

在企业中，上级部门在下级部门间分配年度预算和业绩指标，或者将其他将高级别计划的值分配和分摊到叶成员的工作流程是非常普遍的。SAC分析云的“分配与分摊”功能正是针对这一需求场景所设计的，用户可以基于自身需求，为选定源条件的数据设置分配动因值，在目标维的成员之间进行拆分。 

这里我们先用几个具体的案例来说明“分配和分摊”功能在业务中的运用： 

作为上级决策者，有时很难确定单个产品、地区和部门的成本，但更容易确定总预算，然后根据动因值（如使用的支持时数、人数或建筑物面积）对预算进行拆分。这一逻辑对分摊一些标准不确定、难以量化计算的间接成本（如 IT 费用、管理成本或建筑租金）预算时，通常也很有效。这种自上而下的分配与分摊可以帮助决策者更直观、深入地了解特定产品的营利能力或项目成本，还可能影响经理和员工以降低其团队产生的间接成本。这样的应用场景在公司内部还有很多，例如，根据每个部门上一年的绩效在部门间分配利润；根据当年预算值中的预算比例，分配下一年的预算等。 

对面向市场的业务人员而言，“分配与分摊”功能也能帮助他们分解计划值，制定合理的生产与销售策略。1例如，举行促销活动时，根据目标总销售额在不同产品之间拆分折扣；根据去年销售的类似产品的数量，在推出新产品时分配在其不同门店上架的数量；根据利润率对产品进行组合等。 

根据支持时数向部门分配与分摊 IT 费用 

 

2、“分配与分摊”功能分区更新 

分配与分摊功能，经过2023.07 到2024.10的版本迭代，已由单独模块逐步融入“数据操作”，与“复制操作”“跨模型复制操作”“高级公式操作”三个功能一起构成其四大子模块。 

在SAP分析云中，数据操作、多步操作以及分配与分摊流程都是对模型数据进行结构化更改的灵活计划工具，是帮助计划业务逻辑达成与闭环的重要部分。将“分配与分摊”集成到“数据操作”中的更新，实现了在一个界面中完成完整的结构化设计的场景。

需要注意的是，现有的旧版分配与分摊目前仅供查看和执行，无法再创建和编辑。在系统升级时，现有的旧分配步骤已自动迁移到文件夹“结构化分配”中的数据操作。

 

3、角色和权限 

“分配与分摊”通常由一个用户创建，然后由多个不同的用户运行，运行这一流程所需的权限对应角色的关系如下图所示： 

角色 

所需权限 

流程 

计划管理员和建模者（能设置所有结构化计划流程）、计划员/报告者（能设置分配与分摊流程） 

创建、读取、更新和删除权限 

创建 

计划用户（包括查看者） 

读取和执行“分配与分摊”步骤、流程、计划模型、模型数据的权限、访问模型数据的权限 

运行 

设置完成后，计划用户可以在故事和分析应用中运行这一流程。计划者可能会收到一些提示，让其指定一些成员或值，如果计划员没有收到这些提示，操作则将自动运行这些步骤。 

计划员仍可以通过手动数据输入完全控制数据，但结构化操作可以让你更轻松地执行复杂和重复性任务。

接下来，就让我们一起学习如何创建和运行更新版本的“分配与分摊操作”帮助你提升工作效率吧！ 

 

4、具体创建“分配与分摊操作”流程 

1、从侧面导航中选择“数据操作”。 

2、在“数据操作”的“数据操作设置”页面输入流程的名称和说明，并为流程选取默认模型。其后所有步骤都将基于此模型。 

3、点击上方菜单栏中  “添加分配与分摊步骤”。 

 

4、在“分配与分摊”页面设置结构详情（在该步骤中，可以选择右上角的图标以使表更宽 ）： 

  • 如果需要，可以输入步骤的“名称”和“说明”。 
  • 在“源上下文”下，定义“筛选器”并确定“写模式”。 

需要注意的是，在运行数据操作时，数据操作不会提供应用使用成员的筛选器选项。如果要筛选数据以从分配与分摊步骤中排除某些成员，可以在这个页面选择“+添加筛选器”，使用成员或参数进行筛选。 

如果启用“保留源”，源值将会保留在未分配的成员上。 

  • 在“动因上下文”下，设置“参考维”。 
  • 在“目标上下文”下，选择“填充账户” 和“写模式”。 

填充账户不可用于度量模型。如果启用“覆盖目标”，则将覆盖目标单元格中的任何现有值。 

  • 在“分配与分摊规则”下，选择“源”、“动因”和“目标”维，以及要用于每个维的层次结构。动因维可以是账户或度量。 

5、开始添加分配与分摊规则： 

要添加分配与分摊规则，请选择分配与分摊规则表的第一个单元格，然后根据需要继续 “添加”或 “删除”分配与分摊规则。你可以执行以下操作： 

  • 在单元格中键入成员 ID 或说明的一部分以查看系统建议。 

  • 选择 

     

    图标以打开成员选择器。 

  • 使用表工具栏复制、粘贴或删除规则。要复制规则，请选择“复制”，然后选择“粘贴”→“追加数据”。还可以使用工具栏按钮与其他应用程序（例如 Excel）之间复制和粘贴规则。 

  • 为每个目标维添加多个成员。 
  • 通过选择“显示错误”或“显示警告”或使用搜索字段来筛选分配与分摊规则。 

6、选择页面左上角的 图标（“保存数据操作”）。 

 

5、具体运行“分配与分摊操作”流程 

 

前提条件

设置分配与分摊流程后，具有计划许可的用户可以在故事或分析应用中运行此流程，以加快计划任务的速度。如果要限制涉及的数据，也可以在分配与分摊流程中应用筛选器。 

但运行此流程，还有一些前提要求： 

  • 你需要具有故事或分析应用的访问权限，其中包含基于相应计划模型的表。 
  • 必须为此模型设置分配与分摊流程。 
  • 你需要具有计划模型的执行权限才能运行分配与分摊。 

 

具体步骤 

我们可以在故事中使用“数据操作启动器”微件，来实现本地计划模型计划功能的自动化。添加微件的具体步骤如下： 

1、在故事中左侧边栏的“资产”页面，选择插入“数据操作启动器”。 

2、点击启动器的“更多操作”，选择“编辑数据操作启动器”，打开生成器面板。 

3、在数据操作一栏选择创建好的“分配与分摊”，并设置数据操作的参数。其中固定值会使用默认的参数值，系统不会再提示用户更改，提示则使得用户在运行数据操作时能够设置自己的值。 

 

6、检查分配与分摊作业的状态 

以计划用户身份运行分配与分摊流程后，你可能需要在作业列表中检查此流程的状态。或者，作为管理员，可能需要查看其他用户的分配与分摊作业的进展情况。“数据操作”中的“数据操作监视器”页面集合了所有数据操作流程的作业列表，包括“分配与分摊”流程。 

 

7、结语 

如果你想了解更多关于“分配与分摊”的高级功能和操作方法，可以参考SAP分析云官方帮助文档中的《构建高级“分配与分摊”流程》和《向数据操作添加步骤》《使用“数据操作启动器”和“数据操作”技术对象》10等章节内容，如果想要了解更多“分配与分摊”实践，请持续关注公众号后续推送系列文章。

entry.title: E-Learning: SAP BusinessObjects BI - Hybrid Integration
entry.content:
I'm very pleased to introduce the availability of some new E-Learning tutorials focused on hybrid integration between SAP BusinessObjects BI and SAP Analytics Cloud (and coming soon SAP Datasphere).

Many customers already have a significant investment in SAP BusinessObjects BI, and are maybe not ready to fully shift to cloud analytics within the short term and would rather gradually take on a few use cases in SAP Analytics Cloud.

As per the SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of Direction:

"SAP Analytics Cloud is SAP’s strategic analytics & planning offering and is changing the way our customers
run their business, by allowing them to analyze their data in context and to make better and faster
decisions. The integration of analytics and planning augmented by AI in one native cloud solution allows to
move from insight to action, brings analytics closer to the point of decision, and provides trusted insights from
customers’ most valuable data.

SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence platform (SAP BusinessObjects BI) equips thousands of
companies which reliably manage enterprise reporting use cases at scale. With upcoming versions following 4.3, available on premise and as managed cloud, your investments are safe beyond 2030.
We plan to release the first version of SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 in Q4 2024 where we will continue the
modernization started in version 4.3."

 
This new E-Learning is ideal for customers who are considering the future, and would like to build out some use cases based on a live connection between SAP BusinessObjects BI Universes and Web Intelligence documents using the Direct Connection type from SAP Analytics Cloud.
Aimed at system owners/administrators to guide you through usage of SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect to bridge the two systems

Goals:

  • Create a live connection between SAP BusinessObjects BI Universes and Web Intelligence documents using the Direct Connection type from SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Enable SAML SSO between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BusinessObjects BI
  • Enable Hybrid User Management between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise


Before you begin:

This E-Learning is brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.
Before you can access the E-Learning link, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required with your S-User ID. There is no additional cost involved. 
A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

ACCESS NOW



*Coming soon I will update this blog with another new E-Learning focused on connecting SAP Web Intelligence to SAP Datasphere analytic models using OAuth 2.0 

  • Introduction to the topic – what are we setting out to achieve and why? What is the use case? What are the architecture components involved? Technical requirements?
  • Step 1 - Create an OAuth Client in SAP Datasphere
  • Step 2 - Create an Authorization Server Configuration in SAP BI Platform
  • Step 3 - Create an SAP BI OLAP connection to SAP Datasphere
  • Step 4 - Consume SAP Datasphere analytic models in Web Intelligence

 

Further Support and Empowerment via SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps

As mentioned above, this topic is brought to you by the SAP Enterprise Support Value Map for SAP Analytics Solutions. Compliment your e-Learning experience by joining the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps.
We are providing continued support and collaboration, along with further enablement materials such as Learning Journeys, best practice documents, webcasts, and e-learnings.
The Value Map is a great way for you to access to your service and support entitlements, along with further support and empowerment for SAP Data, Analytics and Planning solutions.

Join the SAP Analytics Solutions value map today.
* S user required


 

 

  • Practical Guide: How to build SAP Analytics Cloud stories using SAP Datasphere
  • SAP Datasphere Connectivity Series
  • SAP Analytics Cloud - Expert Guided Implementation
  • E-Learning: SAP Analytics Cloud Enterprise Planning

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Performance OnePager
entry.content:
The Challenge of Fragmented Monitoring Tools

It is sometimes difficult to perform performance analysis in SAC. It is common practice for administrators to have to switch between our different monitoring tools, each with its own set of statistics, metrics, and visualisations. The lack of interoperability between these tools introduces further complexity to the process. For those without in-depth expertise in integrating and interpreting these diverse data points, valuable insights can be lost in translation, and issues may go unnoticed until they escalate into critical problems.

The inefficiency is evident: instead of focusing on resolving bottlenecks and enhancing system performance, administrators are forced to sift through a bouquet of performance indicators, slowing down their workflow and delaying responses to potential performance degradation.

Introducing a Centralized Performance Dashboard - Performance One Pager

We are pleased to announce the introduction of a centralised performance dashboard which is designed to address the challenges previously mentioned and provides a comprehensive, centralised solution for all relevant performance data, presented in a clear and organised visual format. This Performance One Pager consolidates key statistics from disparate tools into a unified view, eliminating the need for manual data gathering for administrators.

Consider a scenario where, instead of cross-referencing different performance Stories, all of the critical statistics - BI query response times, high Open Story durations, long running Data Actions and Data Management Import Jobs, and large Planning Versions - are available at a glance. This approach allows both experienced administrators and less technical team members to access the insights they need without having to navigate into the complexities of each tool.

The System Overview Performance One Pager

The new start screen of the System Overview will close this gap and display all available performance data that requires attention in a centralized manner. This new home screen is called Performance One Pager. The existing functions of the System Overview will remain, but will be displayed further down because they are not relevant on a daily basis.

The forthcoming releases, 2024.19 and 2024.Q4, will see the integration of the Performance One Pager automatically into the System Overview.

Highlights
  1. Expensive Scenarios
  2. Errors and Warnings
  3. Version Sizes
  4. Details
  5. Integration with existing Performance Tools
Expensive Scenarios

This panel shows KPIs for expensive scenarios where:

  • BI queries run longer than 10s, 20s and 30s
  • Stories take longer than 10s, 30s and 60s to open
  • Log ons take longer than 10s and 60s
  • Data actions and import jobs run longer than 10min

These KPIs indicate that source systems are overloaded, queries are incorrectly designed, users or reports have problems, data actions and import jobs consume too many resources.

The Open Details dialog provides additional information such as runtime history and changelog and scenario-dependent details to narrow down troubleshooting. From the details dialog, the usual performance tools can be pre-filtered to display all details.

The same concept and similar data are available for the Errors and Warnings and Version Sizes panels.

For those who are not already familiar with the SAC Administrative Toolset, please visit the following blogs for additional background information:

  • System Overview 
  • Performance Analysis Tool 
  • Backend Performance Statistics 
  • Data Action Statistics 
  • Data Management Job Statistics 
  • Private Version Statistics 
  • Error Messages Statistics

entry.title: The Case for SAP BTP: The People’s Verdict
entry.content:
After a careful review of all the evidence submitted in the form of product reviews, G2 has announced the people’s verdict on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) in their 2024 Fall reports, and the news is good; SAP BTP has once again been identified by customers as a leader in the Cloud Computing Platforms, Cloud Platform as a Service (PaaS), Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS), and Database as a Service (DBaaS) categories.

This outcome of course is great news for SAP BTP and consistent with prior judgements, which have been determined using a robust assessment methodology designed by G2, indicating a strong level of customer satisfaction with this technology platform. But the question could now be asked, what are the compelling features and outcomes that customers experience when using SAP BTP, which makes the case for this technology platform so strong? The answer to this question is possibly best brought to light by an examination of some recent product reviews, in the customers’ own words.

“It makes everything work together so easily…”

SAP Business Technology Platform is very helpful because it makes everything work together so easily. This makes managing complicated systems a lot easier. We can also connect different third-party apps, and that's possible with automatic processes and smooth data flow…SAP BTP helps us understand things in real-time, so we can make quick choices based on the most up-to-date data. It can be changed and scaled up or down to fit the needs of our business and save us money at the same time. Review from mid-market customer, 19 Sept 2024

“SAP BTP excels at integrating diverse systems…”

SAP BTP excels at integrating diverse systems, both SAP and non-SAP, across on-premise and cloud environments. It provides tools like SAP Integration Suite that facilitate smooth, real-time data flow between different applications, which is critical for businesses with complex IT landscapes. Review from small business customer, 17 Sept 2024

“…it brings everything together in one place…”

What I like most about SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) is how it brings everything together in one place. It makes it easier for businesses to build new things or improve what they already have, without needing to switch between different systems. You can use simple tools to create apps, even if you’re not a tech expert, and that’s really helpful…In the end, it’s not just about technology—it’s about solving real business problems and helping companies grow, and that’s what makes it so useful. Review from mid-market customer, 16 Sept 2024

“…flawless integration proficiency and dynamic automation tools”

The thing I like the best about SAP BTP is its flawless integration proficiency and dynamic automation tools. The platform combines with both SAP and non-SAP systems so perfectly which makes it a great choice for my diverse tech environment. Its cloud native infrastructure is extremely useful for scaling and the security of this platform gives me opportunity to handle sensitive data. Also the customer support is very helpful for any issue/query. Review from enterprise customer, 7 Sept 2024

A continual focus on development and innovation

I can say with confidence, that this news is uplifting to all members of the SAP team involved in developing, innovating and supporting SAP BTP, who continually strive to make this platform and its associated cloud services the best they can possibly be for the customers who use them.

“Thank you” G2, for once more awarding SAP BTP with these wonderful Fall 2024 badges, as well as to our customers too for their kind words.

Until next time, thank you for reading and have a productive final three months of 2024, and if you get time why not take a few minutes to read some of our many other SAP BTP customer reviews.

entry.title: Automate assignment of Users to Teams
entry.content:

  2. Go to Global Settings Page and Enable the Below option under “ Authentication Settings”       

In Database the following parameter will be set to ‘TRUE’ once the above parameter is enabled in GLOBAL_SETTINGS table

3. Now go to your Identity Provisioning Service(IPS) – Target System -   SAC Transformations do the below changes mentioned in BOLD and marked as GREEN

  1. {
  2.     "user": {
  3.         "condition": "($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_TTQT-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_WKFL-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_WKFL-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_TTQT-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_WKFL-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[?(@.value =~ /COM_SLS_ROLE.*/)].value EMPTY false) || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_TTQT-SCAN')",
  4.         "mappings": [
  5.             {
  6.                 "sourcePath": "$.schemas",
  7.                 "targetPath": "$.schemas",

  9.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true

  23.             {

  25.                 "targetPath": "$.id"

  27.             {

  59.                 "constant": "PROFILE:SCAN:SCA_Scheduler",

  80.             {

  82.                 "targetPath": "$['urn:scim:schemas:extension:enterprise:1.0']['manager']['managerId']",
  83.                 "optional": true,
  84.                 "functions": [
  85.                     {
  86.                         "function": "resolveEntityIds"
  87.                     }
  88.                 ]
  89.             }
  90.         ]
  91.     },
  92.     "group": {
  93.         "ignore": false,
  94.  
  95.         "condition": "(($.id EMPTY false) || ($.displayName EMPTY false)) && ($.displayName contains 'COM_SLS_ROLE')",
  96.         "mappings": [
  97.             {
  98.                 "sourcePath": "$.schemas",
  99.                 "targetPath": "$.schemas",
  100.                 "optional": true,
  101.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
  102.             },
  103.             {
  104.                 "targetPath": "$.id",
  105.                 "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem"
  106.             },
  107.             {
  108.                 "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
  109.                 "targetPath": "$.id",
  110.                 "scope": "createEntity",
  111.                 "functions": [
  112.                     {
  113.                         "function": "substring",
  114.                         "beginIndex": 13
  115.                     }
  116.                 ]
  117.             },
  118.             {
  119.                 "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
  120.                 "targetPath": "$.displayName",
  121.                 "functions": [
  122.                     {
  123.                         "function": "substring",
  124.                         "beginIndex": 13
  125.                     }

  127.             },
  128.             {

  130.                 "targetPath": "$.roles",
  131.                 "optional": true,
  132.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
  133.             },
  134.             {
  135.                 "sourcePath": "$.members[*].value",
  136.                 "targetPath": "$.members[?(@.value)]",
  137.                 "optional": true,

  139.                 "functions": [

  146.     }
  147. }

 4.Create a folder along with team creation with the same name below property should be added in the properties of SAC Target System

5.Please make sure "ignore": false under “group” is present. If it is true, change it to false.

6.In addition if the following entries exist,     

 

 

 

 

 

{

                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",

                "targetPath": "$.id",

                "condition": "$.displayName EMPTY false"

            },

                "sourcePath": "$.id",

            },

            },

{

                "targetPath": "$.id",

                "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem"

            },

entry.title: Discover SAP Business Technology Platform at Spend Connect Live
entry.content:
Spend Connect Live and Spend Connect Virtual are quickly approaching, and I can hardly contain my excitement! There you will be able to see the application of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) through expert sessions and customers stories. To prepare, here’s a summary of what SAP Business Technology Platform will be showing.

In the world of procurement, businesses need to find ways to make smarter spend decisions. According to a recent IDC research study sponsored by SAP, Overall, companies saw a 495% three-year return, taking only an average of six months to pay back on their investment by improving cross-organizational efficiencies, creating value through use of data, and more using SAP BTP.

Discover how SAP BTP can impact your Spend systems

If you’re new to SAP Business Technology Platform, Spend Connect Live has several sessions and resources that can help you discover SAP BTP. Our BTP101: Intro to SAP Business Technology Platform session can help you understand how SAP BTP optimizes your procurement processes. You can futher expand on the applications of SAP BTP and view use cases relating to procurement, business network, and supply chain in the BTP201: Explore business scenarios enabled by SAP BTP session. For those interested in Concur, Streamline T&E management with integration of SAP and SAP Concur solutions will highlight how automation and integration between your Concur and ERP solutions can be done using SAP BTP.

Still have questions? You can visit us at the SAP Business Technology Platform Demo Pod on the show floor for additional demos and time with experts.

Hear directly from SAP customers how they benefit from SAP BTP

Now that you know what SAP BTP can do with spend solutions, you’re most likely now wondering how you can apply it to your business. Here are some notable customer use cases you can read, as well as some of the customer success stories being presented at Spend Connect Live.

Customer use case: Infosys, a leading provider improving supplier management

Infosys, an SAP customer, partner, and a global leader in technology consulting, has also improved supplier management. By using SAP BTP, Infosys were able to develop four custom applications for suppliers that allowed the integration of their many SAP solutions. With over 3,000 of their suppliers onboarded, they were able to decrease the onboarding time by 50%—all with one interface. Suppliers are now able to effectively use SAP Business Network for their needs, and Infosys can improve their supplier relations and processes.

Customer use case: Blue Diamond Growers, the biggest almond processor in the world optimizing key processes

Blue Diamond Growers  is the largest almond processor and marketer in the world, and are utilizing SAP BTP to support their over 3,000 suppliers and 1,800 employees by providing real-time data using SAP Analytics Cloud and automating manual processes using SAP Build Process Automation. Together with SAP Integration Suite, they are able to optimize key processes across procurement, inventory management, and quality control.

Spend Connect Live session: How Richemont enhanced supplier collaboration

At Spend Connect Live there are customer sessions highlighting how SAP BTP has elevated their procurement processes. At the session Growing supplier collaboration at Richemont to gain supply chain visibility, you can see firsthand how luxury goods group Richemont has contributed their improved performance to enhanced supplier collaboration using SAP BTP.

Spend Connect Live session: How Endress+Hauser digitized their asset lifestyle

Another customer session to note is

Spend Connect Live session: Sony improves their cost management

Finally, technology conglomerate Sony will be presenting in Optimizing efficiency at Sony with SAP Ariba solutions and SAP BTP, where they will showcase how they utilized source-to-pay SAP Ariba solutions and SAP BTP to improve their cost management.

Learn more about SAP BTP for Spend and more virtually at Spend Connect Virtual

With SAP BTP being a unified cloud-based platform, it can optimize the processes for both companies and their suppliers, whether it be by improving visibility into procurement operations or integrating solutions for a seamless experience. As your business grows, you will need a platform that grows with you—Be sure to attend or watch the BTP101 and BTP201 sessions for the foundational knowledge of SAP BTP for spend. In anticipation for Spend Connect Live and Spend Connect Virtual you can learn more about SAP BTP for Spend at sap.com/btp-for-spend.

I’m looking forward to seeing you all at Spend Connect Live! Can’t make it in person? Register for Spend Connect Virtual here.
